title: High-Molecular-Weight gDNA extraction protocol from moss gametophytes
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fnb9f","text":"The purpose of this protocol is to extract high-molecular-weight genomic DNA from gametophyte tissue of the moss Physcomitrella patens, which contains lots of phenolic compounds. This protocol was modified from a CTAB method for DNA isolation from moss protonemal tissue (Ano et al., 2012). gDNA isolated using this modified CTAB protocol was pure, comparable to gDNA quality extracted from commercial extraction kits. Besides, the yield (69 μg/g FW) was relatively high. The high-quality gDNA is useful for PCR and Southern blotting for the detection of genome editing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":113,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"eiinb","text":"This protocol was designed for the isolation of high quality genomic DNA from moss gametophyte tissue. It might be useful for the extraction of gDNA from other plant species or tissues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Harvest 3-week-old moss gametophyte tissue.

Carefully remove rhizoids from leafy gametophytes. IMPORTANT: Rhizoids mixed in the green leafy tissue will cause pigment co-precipitated with DNA. Be careful to remove rhizoids from leafy gametophytes.

Grind leafy gametophytes to fine powder in liquid nitrogen using the mortar and pestle. IMPORTANT: Grinding moss leafy gametophytes to fine powder is very important. If the powder is coarse, the efficiency of extraction will be much lower than expected.
Transfer the ground powder to a 50 mL conical tube.
Put the conical tube in a -80 °C freezer with cap open until all liquid nitrogen evaporate.
Add 10 mL pre-heated 2x CTAB buffer to the 50 mL conical tube that contains ground gametophyte powder.
Close the cap and shake the tube vigorously to mix CTAB buffer and gametophyte powder.
Incubate the conical tube in 65 °C water bath for 00:45:00 .
Shake the tube to mix buffer and gametophyte powder every 00:10:00 .
Take the conical tube out of the water bath and cool down to Room temperature .
Add 10 mL chloroform: isoamyl alcohol to the conical tube. Mix well.
Then put the tube to a rotator for 00:20:00 at Room temperature .
Centrifuge at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to separate phases.
Transfer the upper supernatant to a new 50 mL conical tube. Add 1 mL 10% CTAB and 10 mL chloroform: isoamyl alcohol to the new tube. Shake well.
Incubate the conical tube at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Centrifuge the conical tube at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 to separate phases.
Transfer the upper aqueous supernatant to a new conical tube. Add 10 mL isopropanol and mix well.
Centrifuge the conical tube at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 to precipitate the pellet.
Decant the supernatant. Air dry the pellet.
Dissolve the pellet in 4 mL TE buffer. Tap the pellet gently until it dissolves completely.
Centrifuge the tube at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 .
Transfer the supernatant to a new 50 mL tube. Discard pellets.
Incubate the tube On ice for 00:20:00 .
Centrifuge the tube at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 to precipitate the pellet.
Rinse the pellet with 70% ethanol. Centrifuge the tube and discard the supernatant.

Air dry the pellet. Dissolve the pellet in 700 µL TE. Transfer DNA in TE to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. Tap the tube gently. Wait until the pellet is completely dissolved. DNA can be stored at this step for overnight at 4 °C .
Add RNase A into DNA solution at a final concentration of 50 µg/ml . Mix well.
Incubate the Eppendorf tube at 37 °C for 00:30:00 .
Add 700 µL Tris-saturated Phenol. Mix thoroughly.
Centrifuge at 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to separate phases.
Transfer the upper phase to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. Leave white stuff in the middle layer between two phases. Discard the middle and lower phases.
Repeat steps 33-35 to extract DNA in phenol one more time.
After transferring the upper supernatant to a new tube, add an equal volume of phenol: chloroform to the new tube. Mix thoroughly.
Centrifuge the Eppendorf tube at 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:03:00 at Room temperature to separate phases.
Transfer the upper phase to a new Eppendorf tube. Discard the pellet.
Centrifuge the tube at 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:03:00 at Room temperature to separate phases.
Repeat steps 40-42 once to remove the phenol residue.
Add 1/10 volume of 3 Molarity (M) sodium acetate and 2x volume of pre-chilled 100% ethanol to the supernatant. Mix well.
Incubate the tube On ice for 00:10:00 .
Centrifuge the tube at 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:05:00 to collet the pellet. Discard the supernatant.
Centrifuge the tube at 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:05:00 . Discard the ethanol. Pellet may not be attached tightly to the bottom of the Eppendorf tube. Be careful to pour off the supernatant.
Air-dry the pellet by leaving the Eppendorf tube in the fume hood. Wait until ethanol residue evaporated, but not over-dry the pellet.
Dissolve the pellet in 100 µL TE. Tap to dissolve genomic DNA.
Weigh 2 g – 5 g fresh weight of leafy gametophytes.
Pat dry leafy gametophytes with clean towel papers.
Add 700 µL chloroform: isoamyl alcohol to the new tube. Mix thoroughly.
Transfer the aqueous supernatant to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.
Add 1 mL 70% ethanol to wash the pellet. Finger tap the tube to mix.
Incubate the conical tube On ice for 00:10:00 .
Add 400 µL 3 Molarity (M) sodium acetate and 8 mL 100% pre-chilled ethanol to the new tube. Mix well.

Store extracted genomic DNA in the fridge until use. Additional Notes: 1. The entire procedure takes about 4 hours. 2. The yield and quality of gDNA from four preps are listed below. FW, fresh weight. Table 1. The yield and the quality of genomic DNA. %s %s Plant Tissue Extraction Method Yield (μg/g FW) OD 260/280 OD 260/230WTGametophytesThe modified CTABmethod69.21±8.43 1.95 ± 0.03 2.21 ± 0.12WTGametophytesNucleoSpin PlantDNA extraction Kit5.81±0.70 1.99 ± 0.02 2.20 ± 0.05 3. Figure 1. The integrity of the extracted DNA. Genomic DNA isolated from WT (lane 2) and the mutant (lane 5) were in high quality. WT DNA digested with BsaI (lane 3) and ScaI (lane 4), together with mutant DNA digested with BsaI (lane 6) and ScaI (lane 7), suggesting that the isolated genomic DNA were free of endonuclease inhibitors. Lane 1, Invitrogen 1 kb plus DNA ladder. WT, wild type.
creator: Lei Zhu
